title: Sarcochilus pictus
taxonomy: Sarcochilus pictus is a species of orchid. Sarcochilus pictus falls under the family Orchidaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Cranichidoideae and tribe Gastrodieae. The genus Sarcochilus is part of the tribe Gastrodieae. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships within the plant.

intro: Sarcochilus pictus, also known as the painted helmet orchid, is a species of orchid native to Australia and New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarcochilus pictus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sarcochilus pictus are teas and infusions, although it is also used in some traditional remedies in the form of tinctures and extracts.
